item.title: Siemens Gamesa, Vestas and others carry $45bn risk from past German offshore wind tenders
item.description: Up to 16GW won by five different developers in 2023-25 auctions may not reach FID stage, Aegir Insights warns

item.title: Equinor and EDF floating wind plans in doubt after Norwegian subsidy vote
item.description: Majority in Norwegian parliament reportedly demands review of planned $3.7bn in support for half gigawatt Utsira Nord site

item.title: The floating wind revolution is delayed: here's why
item.description: Floating offshore wind sector commissioned nothing and saw zero awarded through auctions in 2025 as industry body admits commercialisation will take longer than hoped

item.title: Global offshore wind outlook cut as governments told to fix 'solvable challenges'
item.description: Global Wind Energy Council sets out action plan to treat offshore wind turbines as strategic asset and ease 25GW logjam awaiting final investment decision

item.title: How a Super El Niño could impact wind and solar output in Europe
item.description: Europe is far from epicentre of El Niño events but weather phenomenon could still have marked impact on renewable energy generation – for better or worse

item.title: US wind and solar wins tax credit reprieve weeks before deadline
item.description: A Washington, DC federal court ruled that wind and solar projects have same access to critical investment and production tax credits as other clean energy sources

item.title: New Ramboll CEO ousts Orsted wind veteran and finance chief in reshuffle
item.description: New chief executive Christian Jensby says reshuffle will increase ‘agility’ of Danish consultancy that is a leading player in wind power industry

item.title: EU approves €23bn renewables plan for Italy, as gas-dependent nation opts for CfDs
item.description: The subsidies aim to increase the share of renewables in the Italian grid by 50%, using two-way contracts for difference

item.title: Crown Estate to retender UK offshore wind site dropped by BP joint venture
item.description: BP joint venture and EnBW dropped plans for offshore wind farm at Morgan site in Irish Sea after missing out on power deal in UK’s AR7 auction in January

item.title: The rest is energy: Campbell and Stewart take on Blair, Miliband and Reform over power costs
item.description: Podcasters Alastair Campbell and Rory Stewart sparred over the factors driving UK and European energy prices at Eurelectric's Helsinki summit

item.title: TenneT taps Shell's Dutch chief to be its new CEO
item.description: Dutch-German transmission system operator hires Shell’s Dutch president, who has worked at the oil giant since the 80s

item.title: Mingyang 'absolutely' still planning to sell wind turbines in UK, says Europe chief
item.description: Europe CEO of Chinese wind turbine supplier was 'surprised and dissapointed' by decision to bar Mingyang's offshore machines from UK waters

item.title: Nordex turbine damaged by suspected lightning strike at UK wind farm
item.description: German wind turbine supplier Nordex says it is looking into incident and said that there were no injuries or impact on surrounding environment to site near Manchester

item.title: Three French wind giants are charging ahead with Chinese turbines… just not in Europe
item.description: European developers have commissioned or financed 4.4GW of wind projects using Chinese turbines since 2020, BloombergNEF data shows

item.title: Deals of the Week: Leading wind supplier sold for €3bn, Vestas and Enercon land deals
item.description: Leading wind turbine component supplier Flender was sold by one private equity firm for another, Inox Clean Energy and Siemens Energy both made major purchases

item.title: Merging European turbine giants could work – but the 'wind Airbus' debate misses the point
item.description: Europe may need stronger wind turbine suppliers but lenders will judge any consolidation on contractual certainty, service continuity and the enforceability of guarantees, not on industrial ambition alone

item.title: Trump wind squeeze hits US clean energy as Q1 installations slump
item.description: Department of Defence approvals are holding back 165 onshore wind projects worth $54bn, while battery storage set another quarterly record, according to American Clean Power Association.

item.title: Trump lands 'punch in the face' to wind as installations slump
item.description: Department of Defence approvals are holding back 165 onshore wind projects worth $54bn, while battery storage set another quarterly record, according to American Clean Power Association.

item.title: Developer buys 6GW Indian wind and solar portfolio from rival
item.description: Indian developer says huge portfolio purchase marks a 'defining milestone' in journey to building world-leading energy transition platform

item.title: Jera Nex bp in talks with Germany on future of massive offshore wind sites
item.description: Joint venture of Japanese utility and oil supermajor BP said challenges with projects won at 2023 tender are beyond its control

item.title: New wind turbine order shows Western players still have future in India
item.description: US wind turbine supplier wins new India order amid retreat by other Western players like Siemens Gamesa from market

item.title: How to make money from offshore wind? Build a data centre... inside the turbine
item.description: The offshore wind industry is struggling to attract private financing. The former operations manager for Equinor’s floating offshore wind business believes he has the solution: combine offshore wind with data centres

item.title: This was the moment cross-border power interconnectors proved their worth
item.description: The UK's North Sea and Channel links to Europe showed what's possible last winter – and why more are needed, argues National Grid's Rob Rome

item.title: Why Iberdrola's fraud claim could sink GE Vernova in Vineyard Wind dispute
item.description: Spanish renewables giant Iberdrola and Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners allege GE Vernova deceived them over the quality of wind turbine blades supplied to Vineyard Wind project

item.title: Taiwan's Prime Minister orders wind turbine checks after blaze
item.description: Cho Jung-tai says island's government will inspect turbines older than 15 years after fire

item.title: TotalEnergies CEO's words come back to bite him in US offshore wind lawsuit
item.description: Comments from Pouyanné that TotalEnergies approached US government to strike deal over planned offshore wind farm now being seized on in court battle to cancel settlement agreement

item.title: How Danish government plans 'aggressive' growth for EU's third-largest offshore wind market
item.description: Prime Minister Frederiksen has presented ambitious plans for offshore wind as part of a new coalition agreement but she will need opposition votes to do this

item.title: Chinese wind turbine maker to open factory in Egypt: reports
item.description: Wind power and renewables giant Sany will reportedly invest $300m to open a new factory in Suez Canal Economic Zone

item.title: Skyborn appoints new COO as FID on massive Baltic project looms
item.description: Michael Klingele to succeed Thomas Karst as chief operating officer tasked to scale offshore wind activities and deliver largest wind farm in German Baltic Sea

item.title: Partnerships hold the key to making electrification profitable: report
item.description: Predictable power costs, grid access and stable policy frameworks help projects become bankable, but partnerships hold the key, says Eurelectric
